content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Validate sitemap and sitemap-index namespaces, UTF-8 XML, escaping, location count, size, and required loc values.

hints: For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Sitemap Format model · Theory Sitemap Format, name the owner, authorization, source and emitted revision, Node, TypeScript, URL, robots, unknown value, Fetch, Promise, AbortSignal, browser, accessibility, report, handle, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Sitemap Format precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Sitemap Format model · Theory Sitemap Format, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A theory TypeScript team handles case 9703 during a hostile redirect, stream, and checkpoint drill. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to admit bounded sitemap XML and index graphs through host, scope, hint, and provenance policy; every external value remains unknown until runtime admission produces an owned value; then change one owner permission, seed, URL, robots, sitemap, redirect, DNS, media, or encoding decision and reconcile provenance. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Validate sitemap and sitemap-index namespaces, UTF-8 XML, escaping, location count, size, and required loc values.

For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Sitemap Format model · Theory Sitemap Format, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Any XML file containing loc elements is a valid sitemap.
